summary:
Durvalumab is an FDA-approved immunocheckpoint inhibitor used in treating specific subtypes of non-small cell lung cancer, particularly Stage III NSCLC when combined with chemoradiation. Its role aligns with current standards of care due to demonstrated efficacy in improving survival outcomes for eligible patients.  

Note: The summary above contains redundant content; please revise strictly per instructions—only two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically significant facts related to the query about durvalumab’s application in lung cancer.
